  Department: Department of Mechanical Engineering

  PhDStudent: AJAY KUMAR PAL, NAVNEET KUMAR THAKUR
  ResearchArea: Manufacturing, Additive Manufacturing, Material Joining, Tribological Design of Machine elements: Hydrodynamic/ hydrostatic  journal and thrust bearing), Tribological aspects in Wind turbine, 1. Optimal control of soft and living matter, 2. Computational biomechanics and bioinspired soft robotics, 3. Geometric mechanics and variational integrators, 4. Stability and control of falling thin film flows, Fluid turbulence of plumes and jets, Forest-fires in the context of climate change, Heat transfer in materials science

Summary: This community in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is comprised of Faculty members Abhay Sharma, Arvind Kumar Rajput, Ashutosh Bijalwan, and Samrat Rao. They supervise PhD students AJAY KUMAR PAL and NAVNEET KUMAR THAKUR, and their research areas include Manufacturing, Additive Manufacturing, Material Joining, and Tribological Design of Machine elements. The group's work is focused on various aspects of mechanical engineering, with specific topics such as wind turbine tribology, computational biomechanics, and fluid turbulence.

  ResearchArea: Sustainable machining and grinding: MQL and cryogenic, Ultrasonic vibration assisted profile machining and grinding, High speed machining, Surface texturing, laser cladding, Difficult-to-machine and biomedical materials, Magnetic Barkhausen noise analysis
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 7

Summary: The Community 1 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Dr. Akash Subhash Awale, focusing on innovative machining and grinding techniques. Their research areas include sustainable methods such as Minimum Quantity Lubrication (MQL) and cryogenic processes, ultrasonic vibration assisted profile machining, high-speed machining, surface texturing, laser cladding, and analysis of magnetic Barkhausen noise in difficult-to-machine and biomedical materials. This community is characterized by Dr. Awale's research expertise and his involvement in 7 related projects or publications.

  ResearchArea: Dr. Rajkumar Velu’s research is centered on additive manufacturing (AM) and advanced materials engineering, with a particular emphasis on the development, processing, and application of polymeric, metallic, and composite materials for high-performance and functional components. The group consists of PhD students ANAND KUMAR GANDHAM, MANOJ SINGH YADAV, and RAJARAM KUMAR GUPTA who are supervised by Dr. Pal. Their research focuses on conventional and non-conventional machining techniques, tribology, and materials science, including biomaterials, nanotechnology, and composites.
